articles.title: Woman dies after falling into an uncovered maintenance hole in New York City
articles.description:
The woman parked her SUV right next to the maintenance hole in Midtown Manhattan Monday night and fell in after exiting her vehicle, police said.

The post Woman dies after falling into an uncovered maintenance hole in New York City appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: A woman fell into an uncovered maintenance hole in Midtown Manhattan on May 19.
She parked her SUV next to the hole before the incident occurred.
This tragedy raises concerns about maintenance hole safety for Boston residents.

articles.title: These spray decks have opened to deal with the record heat
articles.description:
Before noon Tuesday, Boston beat record-high temperatures.

The post These spray decks have opened to deal with the record heat appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Boston set a record-high temperature before noon on Tuesday, May 19, 2026.
City opened multiple spray decks to help residents cope with extreme heat.
Residents encouraged to utilize these facilities for relief during the ongoing heatwave.

articles.title: New City Microcreamery expands to Chestnut Hill near Boston College
articles.description:
The new location is serving an exclusive flavor made with sweet cream ice cream, cookie dough, Oreo pieces, and brownie bites.

The post New City Microcreamery expands to Chestnut Hill near Boston College appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: New City Microcreamery opened a location in Chestnut Hill near Boston College.
The shop features an exclusive flavor with sweet cream, cookie dough, Oreo, and brownie bites.
This expansion provides Boston College students and locals with a new dessert option.

articles.title: Kentucky man arrested in Mass. gun store robbery
articles.description:
The shop’s owner was monitoring surveillance cameras during the break-in.

The post Kentucky man arrested in Mass. gun store robbery appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Kentucky man arrested for robbing a Massachusetts gun store on May 19.
The store owner monitored surveillance cameras during the break-in.
Local residents may feel increased concern over gun store security.
